system_affiliation: Tennessee Board of Regents / State of Tennessee
plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: During an emergency, each instructor should establish communication with students, and if the closure is going to be long, construct alternative ways to achieve learning objectives of the course as suggested in the C
catalog_notes: TSU 'Continuity of Instruction / Academic Operations Plan' page under Academic Affairs; provides faculty guidance and eLearn resources for maintaining instruction during a university closure. Part of TSU's broader emergency management program (which also references business continuity and recovery plans and an IT Disaster Recovery Plan).

evidence_quote: An effort within individual departments, units, and divisions to ensure that campus essential functions continue to be performed during a disruption of normal operations.
catalog_notes: WSSU Office of Emergency Management 'Campus Continuity' program page. Essential Functional Areas (EFAs) are required to develop and maintain a COOP using the Veoci platform, which captures critical functions, criticality, peak periods, documents, upstream/downstream dependencies, consequences and coping strategies. OEM directors are Certified Emergency Managers.

evidence_quote: Members are responsible for ensuring that their functional area has a Critical Operations Plan and necessary resources to execute the plan.
catalog_notes: Alcorn State Emergency Management Plan (Revised Fall 2021) organized by Levels 1-3 with EMT/EOT structure. Each functional area must maintain a 'Critical Operations Plan'; the plan includes a Campus Recovery section and satellite/communication continuity provisions. Primarily an emergency management plan with embedded continuity-of-operations elements rather than a standalone COOP.

evidence_quote: The Division of Academic Affairs is committed to ensuring that neither instructional effectiveness nor the safety of our faculty, staff or students is compromised as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic.
catalog_notes: NCCU 'COVID-19 Operations, Recovery and Continuity Plan' (Fall 2021), a university-wide plan with a dedicated 'Plan for Continuity of Instruction,' a Technology Task Force section, and use of the Veoci platform. Developed in response to COVID-19; addresses academic continuity, instructional delivery modes and supporting technology.

evidence_quote: The Jackson State University Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is a stand-alone document, which serves as an operational tool to ensure mission-essential functions and services are maintained during emergencies.
catalog_notes: JSU Emergency Operations Plan (2018) describes a companion Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) as a stand-alone document for maintaining mission-essential functions; the EOP includes COOP activation triggers, references the JSU CEMP, and notes the Emergency Manager as the COOP liaison. Some sections describe the COOP/recovery plan as still under development.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: we prioritize the assurance of academic continuity in the event of an emergency disruption that may result in the temporary or long-term suspension of campus activities, including face-to-face classes.
catalog_notes: Alabama A&M 'Academic Continuity' program (Distance Education & Extended Studies) with Students, Faculty, Staff and FAQ resources and a linked IT Continuity page. Frames academic continuity as protecting the institution's mission and resiliency during emergency disruptions.

framework_alignment: Commonwealth of Virginia IT security standards (COOP/IT DR)
evidence_quote: This policy reflects the University's commitment, understanding, and acceptance of its obligation to ensure continuity of operations for mission/business operations with established recovery time objectives (RTOs) and recovery point objectives (RPOs).
catalog_notes: VSU Policies Manual 6135 'Information Technology (IT) Contingency Planning Policy.' Establishes a Continuity Plan (CP) Coordinator and a Technology Services Continuity of Operations (COOP) Coordinator; ties IT disaster recovery to the University COOP via Business Impact Analysis and Risk Assessment with RTO/RPO, annual exercises, and President approval of the VSU COOP and IT DRP.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: such unexpected events can disrupt face-to-face instruction. Coppin is outfitted with diverse instructional tools that can minimize instructional impact in the event of class cancellations.
catalog_notes: Coppin State 'Instructional Continuity Resources for Faculty' page (Information Technology Division). Includes 'Strategies for Preserving Instructional Continuity' and 'Preparedness Recommendations' (e.g., share emergency plans with students) using Blackboard Learn/Collaborate for remote instruction during disruptions.

evidence_quote: Salish Kootenai College (SKC) is committed to ensuring continuation of essential functions for students, faculty and staff through an effective and comprehensive program of incident management and recovery.
catalog_notes: SKC Policy 705.00 'Incident Management and Recovery.' Lists continuity of operations among the college's highest priorities, requires separate recovery procedures for Information Technology, the D'Arcy McNickle Library, Business Office, Enrollment Services and Financial Aid, and Student Housing, uses ICS and a maintained EOP, with Building Emergency Coordinators and biennial review.

published_revised: 2025 Annual Security and Fire Safety Report
evidence_quote: SBC building departments are responsible for developing building emergency plans, policies, procedures, contingency plans, and continuity of operations plans for their staff and areas of responsibility.
catalog_notes: Sitting Bull College 2025 Annual Security and Fire Safety Report. The college maintains an EOP plus Building Emergency Action Plans, and assigns each building department responsibility for developing continuity of operations plans; annual tabletop/field exercises test these plans. Continuity-of-operations requirement is documented in the ASR rather than as a separately posted standalone COOP.

system_affiliation: U.S. Bureau of Indian Education (federal TCU)
evidence_quote: University departments are responsible for developing emergency response and continuity of operations plans for their areas and staff. Campus emergency management provides resources and guidance for the development of these plans.
catalog_notes: Haskell Indian Nations University (federally operated BIE institution) 2025 Annual Security & Fire Safety Report. Maintains an Emergency Response Plan and assigns university departments responsibility for developing continuity of operations plans, with campus emergency management providing guidance and annual exercises. Continuity-of-operations requirement is documented in the ASR rather than as a separately posted standalone COOP.

evidence_quote: COOP: A plan that outlines the actions and resources needed to continue essential functions during a variety of crisis situations, specifically when the primary facilities are either threatened or inaccessible.
catalog_notes: South Texas College published Departmental Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) template/example maintained by Risk Management. Captures essential functions with a 0-24 hour Recovery Time Objective, line of succession, communication modes, internal dependencies, and college-wide essential functions (leadership, safety/security, communications, basic services, IT). Document_type 'template' per institution's own published guide.

framework_alignment: Florida Statutes Chapter 252; Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan (CEMP)
evidence_quote: Florida International University faces the risks and consequences of natural, technological and operational emergencies that may result in loss of life, property damage or interruption of essential functions of the University community.
catalog_notes: FIU Policy 180.105 'Emergency Management and Continuity of Operations Policy' (published 2020-06-11). Establishes the FIU Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan based on Florida Statutes Ch. 252; assures continuity and protection of essential functions. FIU also operates the 'FIU Ready' unit-level COOP planning tool (Department of Emergency Management), which maintains COOPs for all FIU departments.

evidence_quote: Business Continuity Planning is the process whereby organizations ensure the maintenance of critical operations when confronted with adverse events such as natural disasters, technology failures, human errors, or terrorism.
catalog_notes: TAMU-CC Business Continuity Program (Finance and Administration). BCP is executed after the Emergency Operations Plan addresses life-safety; goal is to continue or resume teaching, research and public service within 30 days of an interruption. Uses the 'Islander Ready' planning tool; business units test plans annually with full testing every seven years.

framework_alignment: Phased BCP methodology (BIA, Risk Assessment, Threat Analysis)
evidence_quote: SJSU Business Continuity Planning (BCP) is the creation of a logistical plan for how the University will partially or completely restore certain interrupted essential/critical departments (functions) within a predetermined time after a disaster or disruption.
catalog_notes: SJSU Business Continuity Planning Master Plan (Section 3.0). Comprehensive program with Business Impact Analysis, Risk (Vulnerabilities) Assessment, Threat Analysis, recovery requirements, testing/validation, ongoing maintenance, training, and records retention. Guiding principles include continuity of student learning, employee health/safety, protection of assets, critical functions and communications.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: this guide was created in case circumstances might require a class to temporarily be offered in an alternative mode with minimal notice. This pertains to a campus closure, increased absenteeism during a flu outbreak, or a family emergency.
catalog_notes: CSULB 'Academic Continuity' Resource Center providing faculty guidance and tools (including a 'Loss of Instructional Time' resource) to shift a class to an alternative instructional mode on short notice during a campus closure or other disruption.

framework_alignment: CSU Executive Order 1014; Federal Preparedness Circular 65
evidence_quote: A business continuity plan (BCP) is a process of developing and documenting arrangements and procedures that enable an organization to respond to an event and return to performing its essential functions as quickly as possible
catalog_notes: SDSU departmental Business Continuity Plan template/document required under CSU Executive Order 1014. Defines essential functions per Federal Preparedness Circular 65, includes Essential Functions Worksheets with maximum downtime (RTO), Key Personnel and Vendor contact appendices, plan maintenance/review log, and BCP Coordinator approval. Note: cites FPC-65 (an essential-functions framework), not FCD-1.

framework_alignment: CSU Policy/Executive Order 1014 (CSU System Continuity Program)
evidence_quote: This program encompasses academic, business, and information technology (IT) continuity of operations plans (COOPs) which support the safety of students, staff, and faculty while also enabling the earliest possible recovery and return to regular university operations after an emergency.
catalog_notes: CSU Fullerton Continuity Program dated January 1, 2026 (82 pages). Comprehensive program with Delegation of Authority, Risk Assessment, Business Impact Analysis with RTO, Essential Functions & Critical Systems, business-unit COOPs, a Multi-Year Training & Exercise Plan, a Crisis Communication Plan, a Business Unit Continuity Plan Template (Appendix D), and CSU Policy 1014 (Annex C). Covers academic, business and IT continuity.
